title: Planchonella pinifolia
taxonomy: Planchonella pinifolia is classified within the family Sapotaceae. It belongs to the genus Planchonella and the order Ebenales. The family Sapotaceae is a part of the clade Asterids, which falls under the larger clade Angiosperms. Planchonella pinifolia is native to Australia, specifically in the tropical regions of Queensland. This species is closely related to other Sapotaceae members, which are primarily native to tropical Africa and Asia.

intro: Planchonella pinifolia, also known as the Australian cherry or green cherry, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to eastern Australia. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Planchonella pinifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
